headline: Bidayuh Cultural Heritage Sites: 23 Baruk Headhouses Demolished Since 2010
context: The Bidayuh community in Sarawak has lost 23 traditional baruk (headhouses) to development and neglect since 2010, with no federal or state preservation programme in place.

cards.big: The baruk is the ceremonial heart of Bidayuh culture.
cards.sub: 23 have been demolished or collapsed since 2010. No replacement or preservation programme exists.

cards.big: Sarawak's Heritage Ordinance 2019 covers 'movable and immovable heritage.'
cards.sub: No baruk has been gazetted under the ordinance. Community nominations have been ignored.

cards.big: Bidayuh comprise 8% of Sarawak's population — approximately 215,000 people.
cards.sub: The community's political leverage is limited despite its size.

cards.big: Longhouse tourism in Sarawak generates RM180M annually.
cards.sub: Baruk preservation could be funded by tourism levies at approximately RM2.4M per year.

cards.big: A culture losing its ceremonial buildings at 2 per year with no preservation plan. In 30 years, the heritage will be photographs.

Indigenous architectural heritage is irreplaceable. A RM2.4M annual preservation budget — fundable from existing tourism revenue — could reverse the decline. The cost is trivial; the loss is permanent.
